tools.note: Apple returns 204 with no response document; the MCP success envelope is synthesized locally.

optionalParameterClassifications.reason: Version creation and localization creation are separate MCP operations with independent validation and recovery.
Build attachment is intentionally handled by app_versions_attach_build after version creation.

optionalParameterClassifications.reason: Legacy experiments are not among the fixed included relationships for the version list.

fields.localRole: Strict Apple continuation URL for this exact collection. The caller must repeat every originating list control, including the effective/default limit, filters, sort, include, fields, and nested limits when supported; the exact query and a non-empty cursor are validated before network access.

tools.note: states preserves deprecated filter[appStoreState] compatibility; app_version_states exposes current filter[appVersionState]. Pagination preserves all supplied filters, page size, and the fixed included relationships.

operations.condition: Always used to validate versionString and releasable state before the irreversible request.

optionalParameterClassifications.reason: The irreversible-action preflight reads only fixed version attributes and does not expand relationships.

operations.condition: Determines whether to create review details or update the existing resource.

optionalParameterClassifications.reason: The existence lookup needs only the review-detail resource identifier and does not expand relationships.
Review attachments are managed by ReviewAttachmentsWorker and are not included in this existence lookup.

operations.condition: Used when the version has no App Store review detail resource.
Used when review details already exist; the detail id is derived from the supporting GET response.

inputs.localRole: Use the existing review detail ID returned by the related-resource lookup.
Copy the related-resource response ID into the update resource identifier.

fields.omissionReason: Apple review detail requests do not accept an attachment asset id; use review_attachments_upload against the review detail resource.

tools.note: The handler uses the dedicated related-resource GET and never emits attachmentFileId, which is absent from Apple 4.4.1. Every nullable attribute preserves omitted versus explicit MCP null semantics in both create and update payloads.

operations.condition: Always validates the version's owning app before creating a review submission.

tools.note: All nullable update attributes preserve omitted versus explicit MCP null semantics; output exposes appVersionState separately from deprecated appStoreState.

operations.condition: Used only when app_info_id is omitted; resolves the owning app and current appVersionState from legacy version_id.

optionalParameterClassifications.reason: Legacy resolution uses a fixed sparse version projection and does not expand relationships.
Legacy experiments are not included during App Info resolution.

operations.condition: Used only for legacy version_id resolution; selects exactly one AppInfo whose state equals the version state or an explicit documented state mapping.

optionalParameterClassifications.reason: Legacy resolution reads only App Info state and does not expand relationships.
App Info localizations are not included during age-rating declaration resolution.

inputs.localRole: Use the owning app ID resolved from the App Store version relationship.

operations.condition: Uses authoritative app_info_id directly when provided; otherwise uses the unique exact-state AppInfo resolved from version_id.

inputs.localRole: Prefer the public app_info_id; legacy fallback is accepted only after unique exact or explicitly mapped state matching.

optionalParameterClassifications.reason: Apple 4.4.1 exposes the current ageRatingOverrideV2 field separately; the MCP age_rating_override input targets that current field.

inputs.localRole: Address the age rating declaration returned by the AppInfo relationship lookup.
Copy the resolved age rating declaration ID into the update resource identifier.

tools.note: Apple 4.4.1 owns age ratings under AppInfo, not AppStoreVersion. app_info_id is authoritative; version_id is a compatibility fallback that never mutates without a unique compatible state match. Every nullable questionnaire field preserves omission versus explicit MCP null, and deprecated intensity values remain legacy-only compatibility inputs.

inputs.localRole: Copy the public phased release ID into the JSON:API update resource identifier.
